title: CI Matrices, Required Checks, Caching, and Merge Policy · Signal Triage
summary: Build a secure required-check workflow across supported environments. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a required CI quality-gate workflow and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate trusted branch workflows from untrusted pull-request code and secrets.
Order cheap discriminating checks before expensive matrices while preserving useful parallelism.
Test declared runtime, operating-system, database, and feature support intentionally.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
Use a changed debug case derived from this module: gates ci required. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Event Trust trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate trusted branch workflows from untrusted pull-request code and secrets.

content.text: For Event Trust, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Event Trust precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed debug case derived from this module: gates ci required. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: All GitHub Actions events have the same trust model.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a gates-required-status-1cf01o: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Required Status into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-required-status-1cf01o:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Required Status evidence cycle before implementing it.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Required Status applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Connect stable named checks to branch protection and define cancellation and rerun policy.
